page: Microsoft Azure D8_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv5 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The virtual machine features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks place the D8_v5 consistently in the average tier for general-purpose workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1532.0 and a multi-core score of 5707.0. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 415.63 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 50.06 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. While it handles tasks like Redis SET operations at 7.82 million operations per second and static web serving at 10.44 GB/sec, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25% at 27.96 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D8_v5 offers a standard resource density suitable for mainstream enterprise applications without the premium cost of specialized memory or storage-optimized hardware. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is recommended for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, application servers, database operations, and basic containerized microservices.
description: The Microsoft Azure D8_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor operating at 2.8 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results position this instance in the average tier for most operations, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 5707.0 and a static web serving throughput of 10.44 GB/sec, though multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak. Offering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D8_v5 provides a cost-effective profile for standard enterprise workloads. It is suitable for web serving, application hosting, and moderate database operations that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8_v5 is an 8-vCPU general-purpose VM with 32 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor for balanced enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering reliable mid-tier compute and memory performance for standard enterprise applications and web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB RAM
Balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for general-purpose applications
Delivers average-tier performance across standard compute and memory benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included, requiring external storage attachment
Recommended for web serving, application hosting, and database operations
